The ark was a symbol of Christ, and, as such, an indication of God’s presence. That gracious promise, Lo, I am with you always, even until the end of the world, effectively brings the ark into our religious gatherings if we plead that promise through faith and prayer; and this is what we should earnestly desire. When Christ is formed in a soul, with the law written in the heart, the ark of the covenant settled there, so that it becomes the temple of the Holy Ghost, there is true satisfaction in that soul.

God took possession of the temple; he filled it with a cloud. In this way, he signified his acceptance of this temple, to be the same to him as the tabernacle of Moses was, and assured his people that he would be the same within it. If we want God to dwell in our hearts, we must leave room for him; everything else must give way. The Word became flesh; and when he comes to his temple, like a refiner’s fire, who can endure the day of his coming? May he prepare us for that day.
